behavior_summary: U8P_SKULLHEA provides two gameplay behaviors: an examine ('U8P_SKULLHEA::look') handler that displays the Skull's description, and a use handler that can trigger a localized earthquake/teleport sequence when applied to a specific target type.
Examining the Skull: the U8P_SKULLHEA::look handler presents the static description string 'The Skull of Quakes ' to the viewing process.

Using the Skull: the use handler first guards on the Skull's quantity and the presence and type of a target. If the target type equals 0x0325, it rearranges item/NPC positions, starts a camera quake, plays earthquake sound effects, runs an NPC animation, fades the player's view to black, teleports the player (and later restores the target to saved coordinates), sets a global 'postEarthQuake' flag, fades back in, and stops the quake.
notable_control_flow: U8P_SKULLHEA::use enforces guards early: it returns immediately if the Skull's quantity is zero or if there is no valid target.
Type check: use proceeds only when the target's type equals 0x0325; otherwise no quake sequence runs.

Sequence ordering: after placement/move operations (Item::push/Item::pop and Item::setQ), an ordered cinematic sequence is executed: Camera::startQuake -> playSFX (twice) -> Npc::doAnim -> process::FadeToBlack -> Npc::teleport (player) -> set global postEarthQuake -> Npc::teleport (restore saved target) -> process::FadeFromBlack -> Camera::stopQuake.
The handler uses suspend/imply style calls around key operations (SFX/animation/teleport) to wait for those actions to complete before continuing.
open_questions: Exact semantics and argument ordering of Item::push and Item::pop are inferred from call patterns but not fully certain (e.g., whether pop places the pushed item into coordinates or moves the target).
The meaning of type id 0x0325 (what in-game object/class this constant corresponds to) is not identified here and is inferred only as 'the correct target' for the Skull.
Which specific NPC/process is animated by Npc::doAnim in the sequence (likely the player or a nearby NPC) is not explicitly named in the disassembly excerpt.

Whether the teleport destination is the player's current map (Npc::getMap used to fetch it) and the precise mapping between saved X/Y/Z locals and teleport arguments is inferred rather than explicitly documented.

main_function.name: U8P_SKULLHEA::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the examine/U8P_SKULLHEA::look event for the Skull of Quakes by presenting a short descriptive string to the inspecting process.
entry_conditions: Event 0: called when a process/player examines or 'looks at' the Skull item.

function_map.name: U8P_SKULLHEA::look
function_map.role: Examine handler that displays the Skull's description via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_SKULLHEA::use
function_map.role: Use handler that, when applied to a specific target type (0x0325) and if the Skull has quantity, triggers an earthquake/teleport cinematic and sets a global flag.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: External API to display short text or messages for an item/process (used by U8P_SKULLHEA::look and by various items elsewhere).

function_map.name: process::target
function_map.role: Return the current target of the using process (used by use handler).

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Return an object's type id (used to test for 0x0325).

function_map.name: Camera::startQuake
function_map.role: Start camera quake effect for earthquake sequence.

external_calls.purpose: Show the Skull's descriptive text to the inspecting process.
Check the Skull's quantity and abort use if zero.
Obtain the current target of the using process; required for subsequent type and coordinate checks.
Verify the target's type equals 0x0325 before initiating the quake sequence.
Temporarily push/place the skull into an item/context stack before movement/pop operation (inferred part of relocation).
Read target X coordinate to save for later teleport/restore.
Read target Y coordinate to save for later teleport/restore.
Read target Z coordinate (adjusted by code) to save for later teleport/restore.
Place/move the skull (or target) to computed X/Y/Z coordinates as part of the sequence (exact arg ordering inferred).
Set the Skull's quantity to 1 after the pop/move operation.
Begin camera quake visual effect for the earthquake event.
Play earthquake-related sound effects (called multiple times for layered SFX).
Trigger an NPC animation during the quake (likely to convey impact or reaction).
Fade the player's/process's screen to black before teleporting.
Obtain a map id (likely the player's current map or destination) used for teleportation.
Teleport the player/process (and later the saved target) to specified map/X/Y/Z coordinates as part of the scene change and restore.
Fade the player's/process's screen back from black after teleportation/completion.
End the camera quake visual effect after the sequence completes.

writes.name: global [0012 01] (postEarthQuake)
writes.likely_effect: Marked true/set to 1 when the earthquake/teleport sequence runs, indicating that a post-earthquake game state should be active.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_SKULLHEA provides two gameplay behaviors: an examine ('U8P_SKULLHEA::look') handler that displays the Skull's description, and a use handler that can trigger a localized earthquake/teleport sequence when applied to a specific target type.
